title: Good case: checkout before concurrent agent work
description: Reproduce two agents waking on the same issue, verify only one checkout wins, and show why an assignee must run checkout before doing task work.

title: Define what counts as a completed agent-work loop
description: Write the operator-facing definition: issue assigned, run executed, artifact or decision produced, review complete, and issue closed.

title: Good case: structured review after retry fix
description: Retry should carry the original issue, project, billing code, transcript pointer, and failure reason into the next run, then close review with a structured review decision instead of free-form approval text.

title: Add dashboard treatment for weekly completed work loops
description: Show weekly completed loops with supporting counts for active runs, blocked work, and pending approvals without turning the dashboard into a vanity metric page.

title: Make Calendar derive agent work blocks from heartbeat runs
description: Calendar should show work history from run records and issues. Do not manufacture Calendar-only scenarios to make the page look dense.

title: Verify dense overlapping agent runs remain readable in week view
description: Use real parallel run clusters from Rudder Studio to verify overlapping work blocks, not synthetic Calendar QA labels.

title: Layer operator checkpoints over agent run history
description: Add a small set of human review meetings so Calendar shows the difference between planned operator time and actual agent execution time.

title: Run packaged desktop smoke after profile routing changes
description: Verify packaged boot, local profile isolation, startup migrations, and UI reachability after the latest dev shell changes.

title: Good case: explicit blocker on npm latest startup
description: Validate that npm latest starts a local trusted instance with embedded Postgres and serves the UI on the expected port. When the dist-tag points to stale metadata, mark the issue blocked with the exact external dependency instead of burning heartbeat runs.

runProfile.failure: latest dist-tag still points to stale desktop asset metadata

title: Approve release note language for agent control-plane positioning
description: Make the release note explain work loops, governance, and local-first operation without sounding like a generic task board.

title: Good case: cite Studio fixture plan with a Library markdownLink
description: Store the canonical Rudder Studio facts as JSON plus a seed script, then hand off the durable scenario plan with the Library markdownLink returned by Rudder instead of only mentioning a local file path.

title: Teach Skill Optimizer to catch component-vs-scenario drift
description: Capture the correction that Calendar should be a downstream signal of real org work, not a separately fabricated component fixture.

title: Good case: create an agent-private adapter helper skill
description: Document how new adapters should separate persisted config, runtime-only secrets, and workspace execution policy. When Ken needs a helper workflow for his own runs, create it as an agent-private skill before proposing organization-wide skill changes.

title: Publish X launch thread: Rudder as the operating layer for agent teams
description: Ship a concrete thread with problem, demo GIF, why task boards fail for agents, and a narrow CTA for builders running coding agents daily.

title: Write build-in-public post about one month of Rudder agents running daily
description: Use actual Rudder Studio signals: issue counts, run clusters, budget overage, blocked release work, and growth follow-up to make the post concrete.

title: Prepare Hacker News launch packet with repo, demo, and founder comment
description: Draft title variants, first comment, technical objections, and a checklist for the launch window. Do not submit until demo flow is stable.

runProfile.failure: waiting for desktop packaged smoke and 90-second demo clip

title: Post LinkedIn operator story for eng leads adopting coding agents
description: Explain the management problem using a small team example: agents can code, but the team needs ownership, approvals, cost, and work history.

title: DM 30 devtool founders with a narrow agent-team question
description: Ask whether they have daily coding-agent work that needs assignment, review, budget tracking, and run history. Track replies by objection.

title: Classify founder DM replies into demand, objection, and follow-up buckets
description: Turn replies into concrete product and messaging issues instead of letting them disappear in chat.

title: Produce 90-second demo clip from issue to agent run to calendar history
description: Record the product doing actual control-plane work: issue assignment, run transcript, approval, budget, and Calendar as history.

title: Seed two community posts with real operator pain points
description: Post in targeted builder communities about agent team coordination, not broad AI productivity. Include a single question that invites concrete replies.

title: Choose proof screenshots for growth posts
description: Use Rudder Studio pages that prove the scenario: Dashboard over budget, Marketing project issues, Calendar run clusters, and Messenger approvals.

title: Turn growth objections into product issues
description: Map objections like 'Linear already does this', 'agents do not need managers', and 'cost tracking is enough' into product proof points.

title: Add lightweight waitlist capture for qualified builders
description: Collect name, repo/tool context, weekly agent runs, and biggest control-plane pain. Avoid a generic newsletter signup.

title: Publish weekly growth report from Rudder Studio data
description: Report posts shipped, replies, qualified conversations, top objections, demo bottlenecks, and follow-up issues created.

title: Gate Product Hunt launch until qualified builder feedback is real
description: Create an approval requiring at least 10 qualified conversations, stable demo flow, and clear onboarding CTA before Product Hunt scheduling.

runProfile.failure: only six qualified conversations so far

title: Make approval threads preserve linked issue context
description: Messenger approvals should show what issue, run, and agent asked for the decision so the operator can act without hunting.

title: Convert operator chat into issue with approval gate
description: When an operator asks for broad work in chat, Rudder should create a reviewable issue proposal instead of silently creating work.

title: Route monthly budget overage into Messenger alert
description: When Rudder Studio exceeds the monthly budget, the operator should see a clear Messenger alert with cost by project and resume options.

title: Make dashboard surface real operational tension
description: Show active runs, budget pressure, blocked growth work, pending approval, and weekly completed work loops in one scan.

title: Improve issue filters for project-first review
description: Operators should be able to inspect Marketing, Release, and Calendar work without losing the cross-org picture.

title: Expose run intelligence for a single issue
description: The issue detail page should show recent runs, retry lineage, context snapshot, cost, and transcript pointer.

runProfile.failure: transcript pointer missing for two local process runs

title: Bad case: mention treated as self-assignment
description: A QA agent sees a plain @mention and starts implementation without explicit ownership transfer. This record teaches that mentions request attention; checkout and ownership still control task work.

runProfile.failure: plain mention did not transfer ownership; assignee remained Linus

title: Bad case: free-form review comment without decision
description: A reviewer writes 'looks fine' in a comment but never records `rudder issue review --decision approve`. The issue stays in review because free-form text is not the durable review outcome.

title: Bad case: durable handoff left as local artifact path
description: A plan is completed but the comment only says `/tmp/rudder-plan.md`. The correction is to store durable project work in the Library and cite the returned markdownLink.

title: Bad case: follow-up issue created without parent
description: A chat-derived follow-up is created as a top-level task even though it belongs under the intake issue. The correction is to set parentId and goalId so delegation remains inspectable.

title: Bad case: skills sync replaced useful enabled skills
description: An agent uses `skills sync` to add one helper and accidentally replaces the optional enabled-skill set. The correction is to use `skills enable` for additive changes and reserve sync for intentional replacement.

runProfile.failure: sync replaced unrelated enabled skills instead of adding one helper
